parts.label: (a)
(b)

rubric.criterion: favorites is assigned a new ArrayList<Song>() inside buildFavorites before any elements are added, clearing stale contents from prior calls.

rubric.point_label: Loop iterates over all songs without bounds error
rubric.criterion: A for or enhanced-for loop visits every element of songs; an index-based loop uses i < songs.size() (not <= songs.size()), and elements are accessed with songs.get(i).
A song is added to favorites only when getRating() >= minRating; songs with a lower rating are skipped.
countFavoriteTitles calls buildFavorites(songs, minRating) — the method written in part (a) — rather than reimplementing the filtering logic inline.
countFavoriteTitles returns the sum of getTitle().length() over every song in favorites after buildFavorites has populated it; returns 0 when favorites is empty.

test_cases.description: Basic: two of four songs qualify with rating >= 4
Edge: no songs meet the threshold of rating >= 6
Boundary: all four songs qualify with minRating = 1

parts.label: (a)
(b)

rubric.criterion: longSessions is assigned a new ArrayList<Session>() at the top of buildLongSessions, preventing stale data from a prior call from remaining in the list.

rubric.point_label: Loop iterates over all sessions without bounds error
rubric.criterion: A loop visits every element of sessions; an index-based loop correctly uses i < sessions.size() and accesses elements with sessions.get(i), with no off-by-one that skips the first or last element.
A session is added to longSessions only when getDuration() >= minDuration; sessions with a shorter duration are not added.
getTotalLongDuration calls buildLongSessions(sessions, minDuration) — the method written in part (a) — rather than re-filtering sessions with a new loop inside part (b).
getTotalLongDuration returns the arithmetic sum of getDuration() for every session in longSessions after buildLongSessions runs; returns 0 when longSessions is empty.

test_cases.description: Basic: three of five sessions meet the 40-minute threshold
Edge: no sessions meet the 100-minute threshold
Boundary: all three sessions qualify when minDuration equals the shortest duration

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt:
Write the PhraseAnalyzer method buildShortWordList. The method receives a String phrase and an integer maxLength. It should repopulate the instance variable shortWords so that it contains, in order, every word in phrase whose length is less than or equal to maxLength. Words with length greater than maxLength are excluded. Any previously stored contents of shortWords must be replaced.

You must parse phrase using indexOf and substring. Do NOT use String.split or any method not on the AP Java Quick Reference.

Complete the method below:

public void buildShortWordList(String phrase, int maxLength)

parts.label: (b)

rubric.criterion: shortWords is assigned a new ArrayList<String>() at the start of buildShortWordList, replacing any previously stored words before the new traversal begins.

rubric.point_label: Loop parses all words including the final word
rubric.criterion: A loop uses indexOf(" ") and substring to extract each space-delimited word and advance past the space. The last word (remaining after the final space, or the sole word when there are no spaces) is also extracted and evaluated — not silently dropped.
Each extracted word is added to shortWords only when word.length() <= maxLength; words whose length exceeds maxLength are skipped.
countShortWords calls buildShortWordList(phrase, maxLength) — the method written in part (a) — rather than re-parsing and filtering phrase with new logic inside part (b).
countShortWords returns shortWords.size() (with parentheses) after calling buildShortWordList; returns 0 when no words in the phrase satisfy the length constraint.

test_cases.description: Basic: two of five words have length at most 3
test_cases.setup_java:
PhraseAnalyzer pa = new PhraseAnalyzer();
System.out.println(pa.countShortWords("the quick brown fox jumps", 3));

test_cases.description: Boundary: all four words have length exactly at or below maxLength 3
test_cases.setup_java:
PhraseAnalyzer pa = new PhraseAnalyzer();
System.out.println(pa.countShortWords("cat sat on mat", 3));

test_cases.description: Mixed: first word too long, last two words qualify
test_cases.setup_java:
PhraseAnalyzer pa = new PhraseAnalyzer();
System.out.println(pa.countShortWords("programming is fun", 3));

prompt:
A music streaming service maintains a catalog of songs. Each Song has a title and a play count. The MusicStore class manages the catalog using a private ArrayList<Song> field named catalog. You will write two methods for the MusicStore class.

Class information for this question:

Song
  Song(String title, int plays)      constructs a Song with the given title and play count
  String getTitle()                  returns the title
  int getPlays()                     returns the play count

MusicStore
  MusicStore(ArrayList<Song> songs)  constructs a MusicStore; catalog is initialized with all songs in songs
  ArrayList<Song> getHits(int minPlays)   to be implemented in part (a)
  String hitReport(int minPlays)          to be implemented in part (b)

parts.label: (a)
(b)
parts.prompt:
Write the MusicStore method hitReport. The method must call getHits to obtain the qualifying songs and return a single String consisting of their titles separated by ", " (a comma followed by a space). If no songs qualify, the method returns "No hits".

You must call getHits in your solution. A solution that does not call getHits will not receive full credit.

Complete the method below:

public String hitReport(int minPlays)

rubric.criterion: getHits creates and assigns a new ArrayList<Song>() to a local variable; leaving it null or omitting initialization loses this point

rubric.point_label: Loop iterates all catalog elements without bounds error
rubric.criterion: An enhanced-for loop over catalog, or an indexed loop with bound strictly less than catalog.size(), visits every element exactly once; using <= catalog.size() or stopping early loses this point
A song is added to the result list only when s.getPlays() >= minPlays; using strictly greater than (>) loses this point

rubric.point_label: hitReport calls getHits rather than reimplementing filtering
rubric.criterion: Part (b) explicitly invokes getHits(minPlays) and operates on its return value; reimplementing the loop and conditional inline forfeits this point
Returns titles joined by ", " when the list is non-empty; returns the exact string "No hits" when the list is empty

test_cases.description: basic — two of three songs qualify
no songs qualify — returns No hits
boundary — all songs qualify at exact minPlays value

prompt:
An online retailer manages a customer's shopping cart. Each Item stores a name, a quantity, and a unit price. The ShoppingCart class manages the cart using a private ArrayList<Item> field named cart. You will write two methods for the ShoppingCart class.

Class information for this question:

Item
  Item(String name, int quantity, double unitPrice)    constructs an Item
  String getName()         returns the item name
  int getQuantity()        returns the quantity
  double getUnitPrice()    returns the unit price

ShoppingCart
  ShoppingCart(ArrayList<Item> items)                  constructs a ShoppingCart; cart is initialized with all items in items
  ArrayList<Item> getExpensiveItems(double threshold)  to be implemented in part (a)
  double totalExpensiveCost(double threshold)          to be implemented in part (b)

parts.label: (a)
(b)
parts.prompt:
Write the ShoppingCart method totalExpensiveCost. The method must call getExpensiveItems to obtain the qualifying items and return the sum of all their total costs (quantity multiplied by unit price). If no items qualify, the method returns 0.0.

You must call getExpensiveItems in your solution. A solution that does not call getExpensiveItems will not receive full credit.

Complete the method below:

public double totalExpensiveCost(double threshold)

rubric.criterion: getExpensiveItems creates and assigns a new ArrayList<Item>() to a local variable; returning null or skipping initialization loses this point

rubric.point_label: Loop iterates all cart elements without bounds error
rubric.criterion: An enhanced-for loop over cart, or an indexed loop with bound strictly less than cart.size(), visits every element exactly once; using <= cart.size() or stopping early loses this point

rubric.point_label: Conditional check uses strictly greater than threshold
rubric.criterion: An item is added only when getQuantity() * getUnitPrice() > threshold; using >= threshold loses this point because the boundary case must be excluded

rubric.point_label: totalExpensiveCost calls getExpensiveItems rather than reimplementing filtering
rubric.criterion: Part (b) explicitly invokes getExpensiveItems(threshold) and accumulates from its return value; duplicating the filter loop inline forfeits this point
totalExpensiveCost returns the correct double sum of getQuantity() * getUnitPrice() for all qualifying items; returns 0.0 when no items qualify

test_cases.description: basic — two of three items exceed threshold
no items qualify — returns 0.0
boundary — item exactly at threshold is excluded, one item above qualifies

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Write the method addLongWords(String phrase, int minLen). The method must traverse the given phrase, extract each word using indexOf(" ") and substring, and add to the words field every word whose length is greater than or equal to minLen. The method returns the number of words added by this call. The order of additions must match the order in which words appear in the phrase. If phrase is the empty string, no words are added and 0 is returned.

parts.label: (b)
parts.prompt: Write the method collectFromPhrases(ArrayList<String> phrases, int minLen). The method must process each phrase in phrases (in order) by delegating to addLongWords from part (a), and return the total number of words added across all phrases. You must call addLongWords; do not duplicate its logic.

rubric.point_label: Loop traverses entire phrase using indexOf/substring
rubric.criterion: Part (a) uses a loop that repeatedly calls indexOf(" ") and substring to extract every word, including the final word after the last space. Off-by-one or skipping the last word loses this point.
Part (a) compares word.length() to minLen using >= and only calls words.add(word) when the condition is true. Using > or adding all words loses this point.

rubric.point_label: Returns count of words added in part (a)
rubric.criterion: Part (a) increments a local counter exactly when a word is added and returns that counter. Returning words.size() (which may be inflated by previous calls) loses this point.

rubric.point_label: Part (b) iterates all phrases without bounds error
rubric.criterion: Part (b) uses for, enhanced-for, or while with bound i < phrases.size() to visit every phrase exactly once. Using <= phrases.size() or skipping any element loses this point.

rubric.point_label: Part (b) calls part (a) and accumulates returned total
rubric.criterion: Part (b) calls addLongWords on each phrase and adds its return value into a running total that is returned. Reimplementing the parsing logic in part (b) or ignoring the return value forfeits this point.

test_cases.description: basic single phrase, mix of short and long words
edge: phrase with no qualifying words
part (b) collects across multiple phrases

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Write the method enrollStudent(String name, int score). The method must check the students list to see whether any existing Student already has the given name (compared with equals). If a duplicate name exists, the method does NOT add a new Student and returns false. Otherwise it adds a new Student(name, score) to students and returns true.

parts.label: (b)
parts.prompt: Write the method enrollAll(String[] names, int[] scores). The two array parameters have the same length; names[i] pairs with scores[i]. The method must attempt to enroll each pair in order by calling enrollStudent from part (a), and return the number of pairs that were successfully added. You must delegate to enrollStudent; do not reimplement the duplicate check inside enrollAll.

rubric.criterion: Part (a) loops over students with bound i < students.size() (or an enhanced-for) to inspect every existing student. Using <= students.size() or list[i] indexing forfeits this point.
Part (a) compares each existing student's name to the parameter using .equals (not ==). The method must return false as soon as a match is found without adding.

rubric.point_label: Adds and returns true when no duplicate
rubric.criterion: Part (a) adds new Student(name, score) to students and returns true ONLY when the loop completes with no match. Always-adding or always-returning true loses this point.

rubric.point_label: Part (b) iterates the parameter arrays correctly
rubric.criterion: Part (b) loops i from 0 while i < names.length, pairing names[i] with scores[i]. Off-by-one or iterating students.size() instead of names.length loses this point.

rubric.point_label: Part (b) delegates to enrollStudent and counts successes
rubric.criterion: Part (b) calls enrollStudent(names[i], scores[i]) and increments the counter only when the call returns true; the counter is returned. Reimplementing duplicate checking or counting unconditionally forfeits this point.

test_cases.description: basic enrollStudent including a duplicate
enrollAll with all unique names
enrollAll with a duplicate against a pre-existing student

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Write the DonationDrive constructor so that donations is initialized to an empty ArrayList of Donation objects. Also write the addDonation method that adds d to donations only if no donation already in the list has the same title as d. Return true if d was added; otherwise return false.

parts.label: (b)
parts.prompt: Write the totalPopularCopies method. The method examines each Donation in the parameter incoming and attempts to add it to this drive by calling addDonation. After processing all incoming donations, the method returns the sum of the copies for every donation now stored in donations whose copies value is at least minCopies. Your method must call addDonation; do not reimplement the duplicate-checking logic.

rubric.criterion: Constructor sets donations = new ArrayList<Donation>() (or equivalent declaration-init); not left null and uses the Donation generic type.
addDonation iterates donations using indexed for or enhanced-for and compares titles with .equals(); bounds use < donations.size().
Adds d only when no duplicate found; returns true after a successful add and false when a duplicate exists.
totalPopularCopies traverses incoming and calls this.addDonation(...) on each element rather than re-checking titles inline.
Sums getCopies() of every donation in donations whose copies >= minCopies (using < donations.size()) and returns that sum as an int.

test_cases.description: basic — three unique titles, threshold filters one out
duplicate titles in incoming list are rejected
edge — empty incoming, threshold high

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Write the AttendanceLog constructor. Initialize meetings to an empty ArrayList<Meeting>, then for every index i in dates, create a new Meeting using dates[i] and counts[i] and append it to meetings. Only add a Meeting if counts[i] is greater than or equal to 0; meetings with negative counts must be skipped.

parts.label: (b)
parts.prompt: Write the static aboveAverageDates method. It should construct a new AttendanceLog from the parameters by calling the constructor written in part (a), then return an ArrayList<String> containing — in the order they appear in meetings — every date whose attendees value is strictly greater than the average attendees of all stored meetings. If meetings is empty, return an empty ArrayList<String>. Your method must use the constructor from part (a); do not duplicate its filtering logic.

rubric.criterion: Constructor sets meetings = new ArrayList<Meeting>() before adding any elements; not null and correct generic type.
Loop iterates i from 0 to dates.length - 1 (using < dates.length); only adds new Meeting when counts[i] >= 0.
aboveAverageDates creates a new AttendanceLog by calling new AttendanceLog(dates, counts) instead of recomputing the filtered list inline.

Sums getAttendees() across all stored meetings and divides by meetings.size() using double-precision (or equivalent) so comparison treats average correctly. Empty list returns empty result without divide-by-zero.
Returns an ArrayList<String> containing exactly those dates whose attendees are strictly greater than the computed average, in stored order.

test_cases.description: basic — three meetings, one above average
negative count is skipped before averaging
edge — all equal counts yields empty list

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Write the method recordDonation. The method should add a new Donation to log only if no existing Donation in log has the same donor name (case sensitive). Return true if the donation was added; otherwise return false. Assume log has been initialized.

parts.label: (b)
parts.prompt: Write the method totalFromTopDonors, which records a fresh Donation of the donor "Anonymous" with 0 books using recordDonation, then returns the sum of getBooks() across every Donation in log whose getBooks() value is greater than or equal to min. Your method must call recordDonation from part (a).

rubric.criterion: Loops through log using indices 0..log.size()-1 (or enhanced-for) to inspect each Donation's donor
Compares donor names with .equals; adds new Donation(donor, books) only when no match is found
Returns false when a duplicate donor exists and true after a successful add
Part (b) delegates to recordDonation("Anonymous", 0) rather than reimplementing duplicate-checking add logic
Iterates log, adds getBooks() to a running total when getBooks() >= min, and returns the total

test_cases.description: basic add and sum
duplicate rejected, return values
empty log adds anonymous
test_cases.setup_java:
DonationLog d = new DonationLog();
System.out.println(d.totalFromTopDonors(0));

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Write the method loadFromCSV. The method should clear any existing entries in readings, then parse csv and append each integer value (in order) to readings. You may assume csv contains at least one integer, contains no spaces, and is well formed (e.g. "72,80,91"). Use String methods such as indexOf and substring along with Integer.parseInt. If csv is null or the empty string, readings should be left empty after the method runs.

parts.label: (b)
parts.prompt: Write the method countHotStreak. The method should first reload readings from csv by calling loadFromCSV, then return the length of the longest run (consecutive sequence) of values that are strictly greater than hotThreshold. If no value exceeds hotThreshold, return 0. Your method must call loadFromCSV from part (a).

rubric.criterion: loadFromCSV empties readings before parsing and safely handles null or empty csv (no parse attempt)

rubric.point_label: CSV parsing populates ArrayList in order
rubric.criterion: Uses indexOf and substring (or equivalent String methods) plus Integer.parseInt to add each integer to readings in input order
countHotStreak invokes loadFromCSV(csv) rather than reparsing csv directly
Traverses readings with a valid loop bound (< readings.size()), increments a current-streak counter when readings.get(i) > hotThreshold, and resets it on values <= hotThreshold
Tracks and returns the largest value of the running streak; returns 0 when no element exceeds hotThreshold

test_cases.description: streak in middle
test_cases.setup_java:
TempSeries t = new TempSeries();
System.out.println(t.countHotStreak("70,85,90,95,72,88,89", 80));

test_cases.description: no values exceed threshold
test_cases.setup_java:
TempSeries t = new TempSeries();
System.out.println(t.countHotStreak("60,55,70,65", 80));

test_cases.description: single value, empty reload preserves clearing

parts.label: (a)
(b)
parts.prompt:
Write the method planFromOptions. The method takes an ArrayList<Hike> options of candidate hikes (in the order they should be considered) and attempts to add each one, in order, to the plan. The method returns the number of hikes that were successfully added.

In writing planFromOptions, you must use the addHike method from part (a). Assume addHike works as specified, regardless of what you wrote in part (a).

Complete method planFromOptions below.

public int planFromOptions(ArrayList<Hike> options)

rubric.point_label: addHike: traverses hikes to compute total miles
rubric.criterion: Uses an enhanced-for or indexed for loop over the entire hikes ArrayList (using list.size() with < bound, or for-each) and accumulates each element's miles via getMiles().

rubric.point_label: addHike: correct add condition and append
rubric.criterion: Compares total + h.getMiles() to maxMiles using <= (not <) and calls hikes.add(h) only when the condition is satisfied; hikes is otherwise unchanged.
Returns true when h was added and false when it was not. Both return paths are present and reachable.
Iterates over every element of options exactly once, in order, using a valid loop construct (for-each or indexed loop with < options.size()).

rubric.point_label: planFromOptions: delegates to addHike and returns count
rubric.criterion: Calls addHike(h) on each candidate (does NOT reimplement the mileage check), increments a counter only when addHike returns true, and returns that counter.

test_cases.description: addHike basic: third hike rejected because it exceeds cap
planFromOptions: skips one that overflows then keeps fitting later ones up to exact cap
planFromOptions on empty options list
